name: Dimensional Analysis
summary: A technique for checking equations, deriving relationships, and estimating physical quantities by analysing the dimensions (units) of the quantities involved.
whenToUseIt: Use dimensional analysis to check whether an equation is correct, to derive the form of a physical relationship when you know the relevant variables, or to estimate an unknown quantity.
steps: Identify the relevant physical quantities and their SI dimensions (M, L, T, etc.)
Write the target quantity in terms of its dimensions
Combine the given quantities to match the target dimensions
Check that both sides of any equation have identical dimensions
Use the result to constrain or derive the physical relationship
example: To find how the period T of a pendulum depends on length L, mass m, and gravity g: [T] = s, [L] = m, [m] = kg, [g] = m/s². The only combination giving seconds is T ~ sqrt(L/g). Mass does not appear — the period is independent of mass, confirmed by experiment.
